rationale: Both papers are in the same core setting (customer referral incentives), and the cited 2020 paper develops key mechanisms (reputational benefits, action costs, sender vs recipient incentive structure) that are directly adjacent to the citing paper’s mechanism tests. The citing note explicitly says it extends prior referral-program work on reward design, and its findings even report a test where reputational-benefit explanation was not supported, indicating likely substantive engagement rather than a passing mention. Given this conceptual overlap and likely theory/mechanism linkage, this pair should be kept for full relation judgment.
